text:
Once upon a time in a quaint village nestled between lush forests and the sea, there lived a wise old owl. Every twilight hour the village would listen intently to him spin tales as old as time. One favorite story was about Felix the Ferret.

Felix lived in a cotton candy pink cottage by the ocean. Every morning he would flap his tiny wrists, waking a big sea turtle just so he could race him across seaweed. People loved Felix because he had such a big heart.

One day a gusty wind blew through town. A loud noise startled everyone—an old oak tree hadn't shaken itself free! Well, that night it became Felix's special project. And guess what? He did it!

With tiny claws he carefully unlatched just-right spots. With soft whirs, he retracted just-right feathers. Slowly he unlatched two big branches.

And voila! The sea turtle got his hat.

Even birds stopped talking. Everyone thought, That’s amazing!

Every vacation
